message:
fix(core): secure temp file creation in copy fallback and restore partial truncation for oversized first lines

Interactive commands (commands.rs):
- Replace fs::write() with OpenOptions using create_new(true) and
  mode(0o600) on Unix for the clipboard copy fallback path, matching
  the same TOCTOU race fix pattern applied to auth.rs in a prior commit
- The copy fallback writes potentially sensitive conversation content
  to /tmp, so restrictive permissions at creation time prevent other
  users from reading the file in the window between creation and
  permission setting

Tools (tools.rs):
- Restore partial line truncation when the first line exceeds max_bytes:
  instead of returning empty content (the prior commit's behavior),
  truncate to max_bytes at a valid UTF-8 char boundary and report
  output_lines=1, last_line_partial=true
- This restores the ability for callers to display a useful prefix of
  very long single-line content rather than showing nothing
- Simplify line_end_without_nl and line_end_with_nl computation using
  map_or() instead of match blocks for more idiomatic Rust

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
